description.p: Your exceptional strength and size allow you to hurl other creatures with devastating effect.

Throw Attack. As an action, you can attempt to grapple a creature of your size or smaller. If you win the grapple contest and the creature fails to escape, you can throw it as part of the same action. The creature is hurled up to a distance in feet equal to double your Strength score.
Long Throws. You can attempt to throw a creature farther than twice your Strength score, but you must make a Strength (Athletics) check.

Collision Damage. If you throw a creature at another creature, make a ranged attack using your Strength modifier. On a hit, both the thrown creature and the target take 1d6 bludgeoning damage for every 10 feet the thrown creature travels. On a miss, only the thrown creature takes damage.
Landing. When a hostile creature you throw lands, it must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier) or fall prone.

sheet.description: As an action, grapple and throw a creature your size or smaller up to {{strength*2}} feet. Throw at another creature: ranged attack with STR mod, dealing 1d6 per 10 feet traveled to both on hit. Target thrown creature must make DEX save or fall prone.
